name: wireviz
description: Wiring / cable harness diagrams from plain-text YAML via the WireViz CLI — connector pinouts, cable colors, point-to-point wiring, rendered to SVG/PNG/HTML with a BOM.

prerequisites.name: graphviz-dot
prerequisites.install_hint: Windows: winget install Graphviz.Graphviz (then add C:/Program Files/Graphviz/bin to PATH). macOS: brew install graphviz. Linux: sudo apt install graphviz
prerequisites.note: WireViz shells out to the Graphviz 'dot' binary to lay out diagrams — rendering fails without it on PATH

install.notes: WireViz is a Python package; Graphviz 'dot' must also be on PATH (see prerequisites). Windows Graphviz installers often do NOT add dot to PATH.

integration_test.command:
printf 'connectors:
  X1:
    pinlabels: [GND, SIG]
  X2:
    pinlabels: [GND, SIG]
cables:
  W1:
    colors: [BK, RD]
connections:
  -
    - X1: [1-2]
    - W1: [1-2]
    - X2: [1-2]
' > /tmp/wireviz-test.yml && wireviz /tmp/wireviz-test.yml -f s && test -f /tmp/wireviz-test.svg
integration_test.description: Renders a minimal 2-wire harness to SVG — verifies both wireviz and the Graphviz dot dependency work end-to-end

post_install_hints: If rendering fails with 'dot not found' / 'ExecutableNotFound', Graphviz is missing from PATH. On Windows add C:/Program Files/Graphviz/bin to PATH and open a fresh shell.
